title: Fix canonical sync helper timeout on fleet drift checks
description:
## Goal
Make sync-canonical-doctrine.sh complete bounded fleet drift checks without timing out on the current canonical target set.

## Context
During flywheel-g0qv9, the default sync-canonical-doctrine.sh --check --json path and an explicit-root precheck both exceeded useful dispatch bounds while resolving canonical-root drift. A scoped root/snapshot helper over enumerated target repos succeeded and canonical-root-drift-fleet-check passed, so the close-relevant drift is fixed but the default helper still needs performance/scoping repair.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Run canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.sh --json with the default sync helper and get status=pass, timed_out=false.
- Add or update a regression that fails if the helper recursively scans large repo trees when direct repo roots are supplied.
- Preserve --json output fields used by canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.

## Testing Obligations
- bash tests/canonical-root-drift-fleet-check.sh
- A targeted timeout/performance fixture for explicit-root direct .flywheel/AGENTS-CANONICAL.md lookup.

## Definition of Done
- Default bounded verifier no longer needs the receipt-local workaround from flywheel-g0qv9.
- Evidence references .flywheel/receipts/flywheel-g0qv9/.
